title: Revisiting our bold preseason predictions for 2025-26 Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins secured a playoff spot in the 2025-26 season with 100 points, surpassing expectations under coach Marco Sturm.
Bruins goalie Jeremy Swayman bounced back, becoming a Vezina Trophy finalist, ranking second in goals saved above expected.
Boston's power play improved significantly, finishing ninth in the league, while adjustments are needed for further enhancement.

title: 4 Rhode Island firefighters reinstated, 1 facing charges, after investigation into drug allegations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Four Pawtucket firefighters reinstated following investigation into allegations of criminal activity involving controlled substances.
Lareto Guglietta, Jonathon Sunquist, and Clinton Lyles fired but contesting decisions; Lyles reinstated after arbitration.
Former firefighter Terrence O’Neill indicted on charges of delivering controlled substances, pleaded not guilty.

title: ‘You’d have to ask the voters’: Bill Belichick reacts to Pro Football Hall of Fame snub
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick was not inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame despite winning eight Super Bowls as a coach.
Belichick did not receive the necessary 40 of 50 votes for Hall of Fame induction in his first year of eligibility.
Notable sports figures, including Tom Brady and Robert Kraft, criticized the Hall of Fame committee's decision.

title: Person snuck into Harvard dorm, attacked student from behind, school says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A person unlawfully entered Harvard's Lowell House dorm and allegedly assaulted a student Tuesday evening.
The suspect, described as an unknown male, fled after the victim screamed, attracting nearby residents.
Authorities are conducting an active investigation and are currently searching for the suspect.

title: Ken Rosenthal explains why Red Sox don’t plan to start selling at this stage of 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox maintain roster despite poor 20-27 record, influenced by weak American League and potential Wild Card contention.
Ken Rosenthal suggests holding off on trades, citing possible mid-season improvement and weak league competition.
Key players like Roman Anthony have yet to return; offensive output remains low, hindering playoff odds.

title: Boston broke the heat record at 96 degrees. Here’s how long the steamy weather will last.
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Boston reached 96 degrees, breaking heat records for May and marking the second hottest spring day in history.
Temperatures are expected to remain hot on Wednesday, with highs in the low 90s and potential thunderstorms.
Cooler temperatures are forecasted to arrive by Thursday, with less humidity and more seasonable conditions.

title: 3 stabbed, 2 arrested in chaotic scene at R.I. town beach, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Three people were stabbed at Narragansett Town Beach, Rhode Island, treated for minor injuries, and taken to local hospitals.
Two unrelated arrests were made for simple assault and disorderly conduct during disturbances at the beach.
Authorities are investigating the stabbings, with no suspects identified; multiple agencies assisted in the response.

title: After auction fizzles out, Karen Read’s SUV listed for nearly $100K at Dedham dealership
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Karen Read's 2021 Lexus SUV, involved in a murder case, is listed for $99,995 at a Dedham dealership.
The SUV's auction was canceled due to low turnout; it was then sold privately before being listed for resale.
The vehicle, having a fair market value of around $61,000, requires repairs including a dead battery and missing taillight.

title: Racist and antisemitic graffiti investigated at Natick elementary school
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Racist and antisemitic graffiti, including a swastika and anti-Black slur, found at Natick's Lilja Elementary School.
Natick officials responded immediately, ensuring the offensive graffiti was removed from public view.
Police are investigating the incident while officials plan long-term measures against similar hate acts.

title: Belichick: Tom Brady leaving Patriots in 2020 was ‘absolutely the right thing for him to do’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick stated Tom Brady's departure from the New England Patriots in 2020 was the right decision for him.
Brady joined the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, winning Super Bowl LV and achieving high stats before retiring after the 2022 season.
Belichick acknowledged the Patriots' weakened roster and his own drafting decisions as factors leading to Brady's exit.
